title: Sugar Bytes Turnado's MIDI Control and The Dictator - 4 of 7
description:
Taken from HOW TO USE TURNADO - learn how to work Sugar Bytes' mad multieffect. Only in CM222: http://bit.ly/CM222

Facebook: http://www.facebook.com/computer.music.mag
Web: http://www.computermusic.co.uk
Twitter: http://twitter.com/computermusicuk
transcript: this video will show you how to control tornado with MIDI and get to grips with the dictator the tornado correctly set up in your hosts as a MIDI control defect MIDI can be routed to it via MIDI controller or data on a MIDI track in order to operate the 8 main knots in fact all the controls in the Edit Page which we'll get into later can be MIDI control too but getting hands-on with the main knobs is what the plug-in is all about by default the 8 main knobs and maps of MIDI continuous controller numbers 1 to 8 so if the knobs on your MIDI controller output those CCS turning them will move their corresponding may not if your knobs are assigned to other CCS either change them to C C's 1 to 8 in your controllers editor or right-click each main knob select learn and turn the corresponding controller node to link the two with the 8 main knobs assigned you're all set to perform on-the-fly effects processing recording the results as a MIDI clip in the host door for editing and use within your arrangement as an effect sequence the automatic North riddling doesn't end there though this tornado also has his own automation system built-in in the shape of the dictator click the dictator button at the top of the interface to open it the dictator enables you to design a sequence of automation for all 8 main knobs together the progression of which is controlled by the big slider on the left which can of course be assigned to a MIDI controller using the right click learn function the sake of the automation envelope is defined by points placed along the eight lanes each one setting its main knob to a particular value at that stage of the sequence click any main knobs Lane to add an automation point for it and drag the point to move it two points are required to make an automation transition which glides from the value of one to the next as the slider travels through the sequence by default each new point sets its main knob to 50% the justice value by holding shift and dragging up and down on the point the colored shadow between the points represents the changing value of the knob intensifying as it increases when there's no automation on the lane the main knob drops to zero ie off you can also move and release a main knob to enter an automation point for at the current slider position set the value of the knob when released or adjust the value of a point already at that position the dice and X button is under each lane randomize and clearly respectively while the same buttons under the slider randomize and clear all lanes the types of presets can be saved using the menu at the top which contains a small collection of handy starting points and you can set the dictator to ignore tornado preset changes by activating the padlock button
